case_title: JOE A CARRANZA VS. HYATT REGENCY SAN FRANCISCO et al

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-13-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT HYATT CORPORATION DBA HYATT REGENCY SAN FRANCSICO'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES AND PERMANENT INJUNCTIVE RELIEF.
APPEARANCE: Gregory S. Walston, Esq. of The Walston Legal Group 956-9200 for pltf & opposing party; Margaret E. Murray, Esq. of Folger Levin & Kahn LLP 986-2800 for deft & moving party.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - SUSTAINED AS TO FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION WITH LEAVE TO AMEND IF PLAINTIFF CAN ALLEGE IN GOOD FAITH VIOLATION OF SOME PUBLIC POLICY OTHER THAN RETALIATION FOR UNION ACTIVITIES; WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION; OVERRULED AS TO THE FOURTH, FIFTH AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION. Plaintiff has until 7/7/06 to amend complaint. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. (301/PJB/DM) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189
